svg.text: ━ CASCADE ╌ ╌ SET NULL 🔑 PK ● FK (target table icon) [U] Unique [I] Indexed RLS = Row-Level Security
The scientific knowledge graph captures the AI agent's reasoning process. Hypotheses are proposed, tested and refined; findings record discrete observations; literature references ground the
analysis in published research. Junction tables weave these entities into an interconnected evidence web.
A testable scientific explanation proposed by the AI agent. Carries a confidence score, priority ranking and explicit test strategy. Hypotheses evolve through states — active, tested,
disproved, merged or spawned into child hypotheses.
A discrete observation produced during analysis — from code execution, literature review or reasoning. Findings are the raw evidence that supports or challenges hypotheses.
A scientific publication retrieved from PubMed or arXiv. Stores bibliographic metadata, DOI, abstract and a relevance score indicating how closely the paper relates to the current analysis.
Junction table linking findings to the hypotheses they support or contradict, forming the evidence web of the knowledge graph.
Junction table connecting findings to the literature references that produced or corroborate them.
Records the parent–child lineage when one hypothesis gives rise to a more refined successor, including the reason for the derivation.
All scientific records are scoped to their parent job. A user can read and modify hypotheses, findings and literature only if they own the job or hold an edit-level share. Read-only share
holders can view the knowledge graph but cannot alter it. Junction tables inherit access from either linked side.
